title: GEHIRNs Japanese branch only ever consisted of six people
display_title: GEHIRNs Japanese branch only ever consisted of six people
wikitext:
[]: GEHIRN's Japanese branch only ever consisted of six people             Quote Post by WDS » Wed Sep 28, 2022 1:30 pm 1. Gendo (founding member) 2. Yui (founding member) 3. Dr. Akagi Senior (founding member) 4. Fuyutsuki (joined 2003) 5. Dr. Akagi Junior (joined 2008) 6. The random security guard who interrogated Ritsuko when she got lost on her first day of work I think it's amazing that these six people were able to accomplish so much on their own, without entire teams of engineers helping them! Gendo and Yui managed to build the first Unit 00 mockup all by themselves by 2003, and the Akagis were able to build the Magi all by themselves by 2010, with Naoko flying solo until 2008. I'm still not entirely clear on what Fuyutsuki did other than bitch and whine about Second Impact, but it was probably something REALLY important, like fetching coffee and cigarettes for the Akagis or changing Rei's diapers. (seriously though, as Yui's teacher, he probably fixed all of her fuck-ups and got the Evas actually working , if the results of Unit 00's activation test could be called that)

[]: Re: GEHIRN's Japanese branch only ever consisted of six people             Quote Post by sithsauron » Wed Sep 28, 2022 4:23 pm not only that, but the Unit-00 was totally just built out of cardboard (cannon), totally fooling Fuyutsuki into being hired. It turns out Fuyutsuki was being used by Gehirn to fuel himself with previously un-thought-of jealously levels, to the point it would disrupt the 3rd impact during instrumentality and save the human race, beyond what Shinji alone would be capable of.

[]: Re: GEHIRN's Japanese branch only ever consisted of six people             Quote Post by WDS » Wed Sep 28, 2022 9:13 pm sithsauron wrote: not only that, but the Unit-00 was totally just built out of cardboard (cannon)

The random security guard who interrogated Ritsuko when she got lost on her first day of work I think it's amazing that these six people were able to accomplish so much on their own, without entire teams of engineers helping them!
